summary: Descriptive overview of domesticviolence in substantive law: protective orders, mandatory arrest, federal protections.
key_points: protective orders is a fundamental concept in family law.
Legal standards for domesticviolence are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine domesticviolence in litigation.
The doctrine of domesticviolence has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding domesticviolence requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
